name: RICH & CREAMY LASAGNE
description: This lasagne will ensure you never go short of 'mmms' and dinner guests no end. The béchamel is creamy, the tomato sauce is long on taste; no one overrides the pasta just perfectly cooked. Better still, while party feeds on a budget, this one will serve a loved one that often leave lots of leftovers - what could be better than fresh day lasagne.
ingredients: 4 tbsp olive oil, extra for greasing
A few sprigs fresh thyme, to serve
method: Preheat oven to 180°C | Line 2 large baking trays | Brush the inside of a 23 x 33cm lasagne dish with oil | Food processor | Large saucepan or casserole pan on a medium heat | Small saucepan | Liquidiser
Put the porcini mushrooms in the tomato sauce pan | Pour over boiling water to cover

Peel the squash, cut it in half and scoop out and discard the seeds | Dice the aubergine and cut the courgettes into 1cm thick slices | Put them in a bowl with the chilli powder and 3 tablespoons of the olive oil | Toss to coat | Lay the vegetables out on the two lined baking trays and roast for 15 minutes

Meanwhile, make the tomato sauce | Heat the remaining tablespoon of oil in the large saucepan or casserole pan over a medium heat until hot | Add the onion and garlic and cook for 5 minutes | Peel and mince the garlic | Add the garlic to the pan and cook for a further 30 seconds

Roughly chop the carrot and celery | Cut the pepper in half, cut out the stem and seeds, and roughly chop | Add the vegetables to the pan and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft but not browned | Meanwhile, soak the porcini from the hot water and chop finely | Slice the leaves should easily come away | Put them in the food processor and chop everything finely | Squeeze the mushrooms dry (by hand)

Add the chopped veg to the pan with the onion and garlic | Stir and cook for 15 minutes | Pour in the red wine; increase the heat to medium-high and cook for a further 2-3 minutes, until the wine has cooked off but not letting it caramelise

Take the porcini out of the mug and finely chop | Add to the pan with the liquid from the mug, the chopped tomatoes, passata and the sugar | Season with salt and pepper and simmer for a further 30 minutes

Remove the trays from the oven | Dot some sauce over the base with the béchamel mix and mix well | If the butternut squash is very wet, drain it and pat dry, breaking out each layer | Transfer to a large bowl and set aside

Add the aubergine to the tomato sauce and simmer for a further 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened | Taste and season well with salt and pepper | Take off the heat and set aside
